children.name: Tachyglossus
children.description: Tachyglossus — a genus of echidnas in the family Tachyglossidae.

children.name: Tachyglossus aculeatus
children.description: The short-beaked echidna, also known as the common echidna, or short-nosed echidna, is one of four living species of echidna, and the only member of the genus Tachyglossus, from Ancient Greek ταχύς, meaning "fast", and γλῶσσα, meaning "tongue". It is covered in fur and spines and has a distinctive snout and a specialised tongue, which it uses to catch its insect prey at a great speed. Like the other extant monotremes, the short-beaked echidna lays eggs; the monotremes are the only living group of mammals to do so.

children.name: Tachyglossus lawesii
children.description: Tachyglossus lawesii — a species in the genus Tachyglossus, family Tachyglossidae. Also known as echidnas.

children.name: Zaglossus
children.description: Zaglossus — a genus of echidnas in the family Tachyglossidae.

children.name: Echidna robusta
children.description: Echidna robusta — a species in the genus Zaglossus, family Tachyglossidae. Also known as echidnas.

children.name: Zaglossus attenboroughi
children.description: Zaglossus attenboroughi, also known as Attenborough's long-beaked echidna or locally as the payangko, is one of three species from the genus Zaglossus that inhabits the island of New Guinea. It lives in the Cyclops Mountains near the northern coast of the island, which are near the cities of Sentani and Jayapura in the Indonesian province of Papua in Western New Guinea. It is named in honour of naturalist Sir David Attenborough.

children.name: Zaglossus bartoni
children.description: The eastern long-beaked echidna, also called Barton's long-beaked echidna, is one of three species from the genus Zaglossus to occur in New Guinea. It is found mainly in the eastern half at elevations between 2,000 and 3,000 metres.

children.name: Megalibgwilia
children.description: Megalibgwilia — a genus of echidnas in the family Tachyglossidae.
